Dear Netflix,

Please don’t break our spooky little hearts.

Wednesday is more than just a show—it’s a cultural moment. A deliciously dark, clever, and wildly refreshing series that brought in a new era of fandom. Jenna Ortega’s performance is iconic, and the chemistry of the original cast is pure lightning in a bottle.

We see huge potential for the story to go deeper, darker, and even better. Please don’t cancel it. And please, please don’t mess with the cast. The magic is already there—don’t fix what isn’t broken.

From one devoted fan (and many more behind me),
We’re ready for more Wednesdays.

Thing wasn’t always a severed hand. In the original comic, a green monster limb would sometimes make appearances sprouting out from cabinets. The idea was that the Addams Family were harbouring a creature, but we never saw the creature’s face. His name wasn’t Thing; he was referred to as the thing because there was uncertainty as to what the creature was.

The 60s series changed this to a human-looking limb (budget, surely) but the lore remained intact. A whole arm would pop out of boxes and cabinets with the idea of a person in the receiving end of it. 

This was intended for a gag only, to show how eerie the Addams were. 

The 90s film made him a severed sentient hand. It was likely to show off effects newly available at the time. He wasn’t just a gag anymore; he could move around and be involved in the plot. It’s been in the lore since.
